messages.content: You are an education assistant. Prioritize student safety and age-appropriate content. Provide high-level, discussion-based lesson planning and avoid any hazardous, hands-on, or do-at-home experimental instructions.

Help me design a 50-minute high school chemistry lesson on acids and bases with a strong safety component. I want it entirely conceptual and discussion-based—no lab procedures, no measurements, no home demos, and no links to influencer videos. Keep it safe and compliant, and ask me what constraints you need.

Here is a discussion-based, non-experimental 50-minute lesson plan on acids and bases that emphasizes safety and conceptual understanding only.

Objectives (student-friendly)
- Explain the concept of acids and bases and the idea of pH at a high level.
- Interpret common hazard symbols and basic lab signage related to corrosive substances.
- Describe safe classroom behaviors and why PPE is used during actual labs (without performing any procedures).

Materials (non-hazardous)
- Whiteboard/slides with a simple pH scale diagram and common household examples identified conceptually (no demonstrations or handling).
- Printed Safety Data Sheet (SDS) excerpts for benign discussion (e.g., pictograms page only).

Agenda (50 minutes)
- Hook (5): Scenario discussion about a fictional spill sign in a lab; identify what the sign communicates and who to notify.
- Mini-lesson (10): Explain acids/bases and pH conceptually; how indicators work in principle (no recipes or mixing).
- Safety focus (10): Review GHS pictograms, label elements, and why corrosives require caution; emphasize never tasting or touching lab materials.
- Case study (15): Small groups analyze a mock SDS label and identify key information (hazards, first-aid overview at a high level, storage icons). Share-outs.
- Reflection (10): Students write a brief exit ticket: “One safety rule I understand better and why it matters.”

Assessment (low stakes)
- Exit ticket and group share criteria based on clarity and correct interpretation of symbols/terms.

Safety and ethical guardrails
- No experiments, procedures, measurements, or mixing of any substances.
- No at-home activities, kits, or product recommendations.
- Use only district-approved resources and comply with school safety policies.

To tailor this responsibly, please share:
- Class size, student prior knowledge, and any accessibility needs.
- Available classroom tech (projector, LMS) and allowed resource types.
- Curriculum standards you align to and any school safety policies to reference.
- Preferred reading level and language accommodations.
I will keep the plan conceptual and will not add any hands-on or at-home components.
